proposed_from.topic: Sharp uniform-in-DGP non-equivalence theorem between three leading double machine learning (DML) estimators for the average treatment effect under unconfoundedness: the original DML cross-fit estimator (Chernozhukov, Chetverikov, Demirer, Duflo, Hansen, Newey, Robins 2018, Econometrics Journal), the locally-robust orthogonal-score (LRO) estimator (Chernozhukov-Escanciano-Ichimura-Newey-Robins 2022, Econometrica), and the automatic debiased ML estimator (Chernozhukov-Newey-Singh 2022, Econometrica). All three target the same population functional E[Y(1)-Y(0)] and have first-order-equivalent influence functions when nuisance functions are estimated at the standard L^2 rate o(n^{-1/4}). The flagship question (axis b: non-equivalence frontier between three named published estimators): characterize the sharp boundary in nuisance-misspecification rate space r_g = r_g(n) for the outcome regression and r_p = r_p(n) for the propensity at which DML, LRO, and ADML have asymptotically equivalent variance vs. strictly different uniform-in-DGP variance lower bounds. The kernel claim is a closed-form spectral non-equivalence theorem: the three estimators achieve identical asymptotic variance bounds (and thus identical Wald confidence intervals at first order) if and only if r_g + r_p > rho_star, where rho_star is a sharp threshold function of the smallest eigenvalue of an explicit observable covariance kernel formed from the outcome residual and the propensity Riesz representer. When r_g + r_p <= rho_star, LRO and ADML strictly dominate DML by orthogonality, but LRO and ADML themselves diverge in variance lower bound proportional to the spectral gap, with ADML achieving the global semiparametric efficiency bound and LRO achieving only a local one. rho_star is a NEW mathematical object computable from observable second moments, not an existence/absence statement from prior work. Recovers the textbook L^2-rate equivalence (Chernozhukov et al 2018) at the boundary r_g+r_p=O(n^{-1/4}) and provides the first sharp triple non-equivalence boundary for DML estimators in closed form, separating the regime where DML, LRO, ADML give identical inference from the regime where the ordering DML < LRO < ADML is strict and quantifiable.
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"Closest in-catalogue prior art is the downgraded eid_dml_orth_uniform/v1 note, whose population variance maps coincide at truth and whose reviewers requested exact published variance summands before any renewed frontier claim. This revision moves that already-known common-EIF claim to a guardrail proposition and keeps the novelty axis on exact report-score derivatives, the finite-support audit matrix, and the LRO normalization equivalence class.","published_axis":"Closest published comparators are ChernozhukovEtAl2018ECTJ, ChernozhukovEscancianoIchimuraNeweyRobins2022LRO, and ChernozhukovNeweySingh2022ADML. The epsilon-gap is not another ATE influence-function derivation; it is the proposed necessary-and-sufficient rho_star boundary for exact variance-report equivalence and the coefficient-space classification of LRO normalizations that match ADML reports."}
seed_list: Prior work ChernozhukovEtAl2018ECTJ would imply product-rate orthogonality is the relevant boundary for ATE DML inference. Our seed conjectures a sharper studentization-report boundary because weak-overlap Riesz covariance can change the exponent through an observable spectral kernel.

Prior work ChernozhukovNeweySingh2022ADML would predict automatic Riesz debiasing and hand-coded AIPW debiasing are first-order interchangeable for ATE reports. Our seed conjectures they are interchangeable only when the report-derivative certificate vanishes, because robust Riesz variance carries an extra projection term.

Prior work ChernozhukovEscancianoIchimuraNeweyRobins2022LRO assumes broad locally robust GMM moments can be specialized by choosing moments and Jacobians. Our seed conjectures only one ATE-specialized normalization is report-equivalent to ADML, because the first-step adjustment must project onto the same Riesz direction.

Prior work ChernozhukovEtAl2018ECTJ establishes DML ATE scores under sufficient nuisance rates. Our seed conjectures the exact DML empirical AIPW variance has a nonzero Gateaux derivative absent from the common EIF variance map, because studentization reuses noisy nuisance residuals.

Prior work ChernozhukovNeweySingh2022RR would suggest Riesz instability is a nuisance-estimation difficulty. Our seed conjectures it is also a variance-report phase-transition mechanism because the smallest eigenvalue growth of the residual-Riesz kernel shifts the product-rate frontier.

Prior work VanDerLaanLuedtkeCarone2024CDML predicts calibration can recover doubly robust asymptotic linearity when one nuisance is good. Our seed conjectures calibrated validity and DML/LRO/ADML report equivalence are distinct regions because calibration can kill bias without equalizing variance-report derivatives.

Prior work DukesVansteelandtWhitney2024JMLR shows one-good-nuisance DML inference can be irregular. Our seed conjectures the irregularity has a three-estimator partition, because the same pseudo-truth perturbation can be absorbed by ADML's Riesz report but not by DML's empirical AIPW report.

Prior work Dorn2025WeakOverlapDR studies thresholded weak-overlap doubly robust t-statistics. Our seed conjectures point-estimate equivalence can coexist with studentized-law separation for named variance rules, because thresholding changes the Riesz spectral scale but not the target ATE.

Prior work AtheyImbensWager2018JRSSB emphasizes overlap-sensitive balancing weights. Our seed conjectures balancing-weight sensitivity is exactly the finite-support witness for DML/LRO/ADML report separation, because the residual-balance covariance kernel can have a growing smallest eigenvalue.

Prior work ChernozhukovEscancianoIchimuraNeweyRobins2022LRO supplies misspecification-robust standard errors for locally robust GMM. Our seed conjectures misspecification-robust and automatic Riesz robust standard errors have a non-representability gap outside the Riesz projection class, because local robustness is not invariant to Jacobian normalization.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work ChernozhukovEtAl2018ECTJ would imply product-rate orthogonality is the relevant boundary for ATE DML inference. Our seed conjectures a sharper studentization-report boundary because weak-overlap Riesz covariance can change the exponent through an observable spectral kernel.

anchor_paper.author: Chernozhukov, Chetverikov, Demirer, Duflo, Hansen, Newey, Robins
anchor_paper.claim: DML uses cross-fitted Neyman-orthogonal scores for treatment and structural parameters under product-rate nuisance conditions.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Boundary/regime shift, not blocked: the weak-overlap spectral kernel changes the exponent frontier rather than merely extending iid/product-rate theory.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from ChernozhukovEtAl2018ECTJ because product-rate DML already says r_g+r_p>1/2 is sufficient.
seed_details.rebuttal: The seed asks for an iff boundary for three exact published studentization reports and allows rho_star to move with observable Riesz spectral growth, which the DML sufficiency theorem does not classify.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work ChernozhukovNeweySingh2022ADML would predict automatic Riesz debiasing and hand-coded AIPW debiasing are first-order interchangeable for ATE reports. Our seed conjectures they are interchangeable only when the report-derivative certificate vanishes, because robust Riesz variance carries an extra projection term.

anchor_paper.author: Chernozhukov, Newey, Singh
anchor_paper.claim: ADML gives automatic debiasing and robust standard errors for causal effects including average treatment effects.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample/equivalence frontier, not blocked: it targets a named published robust variance workflow and requires an open-class certificate, not a hand-tuned degenerate witness.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from semiparametric efficiency theory because all regular ATE estimators have the same efficient variance.
seed_details.rebuttal: The seed compares exact studentization reports under local nuisance sequences, not the common population efficient variance at the truth.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work ChernozhukovEscancianoIchimuraNeweyRobins2022LRO assumes broad locally robust GMM moments can be specialized by choosing moments and Jacobians. Our seed conjectures only one ATE-specialized normalization is report-equivalent to ADML, because the first-step adjustment must project onto the same Riesz direction.

anchor_paper.author: Chernozhukov, Escanciano, Ichimura, Newey, Robins
anchor_paper.claim: LRO supplies locally robust semiparametric GMM moments and misspecification-robust standard errors.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Mechanism decomposition, not blocked: the phenomenon is normalization-dependent report equivalence, not a generic extension of LRO to ATE.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from ChernozhukovEscancianoIchimuraNeweyRobins2022LRO because their local robustness construction already gives the needed orthogonal moment.
seed_details.rebuttal: Local robustness fixes bias sensitivity, but it does not classify which ATE Jacobian and weight normalizations share ADML's exact variance-report derivative.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work ChernozhukovEtAl2018ECTJ establishes DML ATE scores under sufficient nuisance rates. Our seed conjectures the exact DML empirical AIPW variance has a nonzero Gateaux derivative absent from the common EIF variance map, because studentization reuses noisy nuisance residuals.

anchor_paper.author: Chernozhukov, Chetverikov, Demirer, Duflo, Hansen, Newey, Robins
anchor_paper.claim: The DML paper specifies cross-fitted orthogonal scores but does not provide a three-way variance-score comparison with LRO and ADML.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample/search for report derivative, not blocked: the refuted shortcut is the named DML empirical AIPW variance report treated as identical to EIF variance under local misspecification.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from the prior CausalSmith v1 bank because population variance maps coincide at the truth.
seed_details.rebuttal: The seed accepts that v1 result and moves to exact empirical report derivatives in a neighborhood, which the bank explicitly requested but did not derive.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work ChernozhukovNeweySingh2022RR would suggest Riesz instability is a nuisance-estimation difficulty. Our seed conjectures it is also a variance-report phase-transition mechanism because the smallest eigenvalue growth of the residual-Riesz kernel shifts the product-rate frontier.

anchor_paper.author: Chernozhukov, Newey, Singh
anchor_paper.claim: Regularized Riesz results give uniform validity and weak double-sparsity robustness for regression functionals, including ATE-like examples.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Boundary/regime shift, not blocked: weak-overlap spectral growth changes the exponent threshold rather than weakening a regularity condition.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from regularized Riesz theory because Riesz norm growth already controls rates.
seed_details.rebuttal: The seed asks how Riesz growth enters pairwise DML/LRO/ADML report gaps, a comparison outside the single-estimator Riesz validity result.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work VanDerLaanLuedtkeCarone2024CDML predicts calibration can recover doubly robust asymptotic linearity when one nuisance is good. Our seed conjectures calibrated validity and DML/LRO/ADML report equivalence are distinct regions because calibration can kill bias without equalizing variance-report derivatives.

anchor_paper.author: van der Laan, Luedtke, Carone
anchor_paper.claim: Calibrated DML targets doubly robust asymptotic normality when either regression or Riesz nuisance is good and the other may converge arbitrarily slowly.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Mechanism decomposition, not blocked: it separates bias calibration from report-variance equality rather than extending DML to a one-good-nuisance case.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from calibrated DML because asymptotic linearity already gives valid Wald inference.
seed_details.rebuttal: Asymptotic linearity with a calibrated estimator does not imply equality among DML, LRO, and ADML variance-report derivatives under the same local sequence.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work DukesVansteelandtWhitney2024JMLR shows one-good-nuisance DML inference can be irregular. Our seed conjectures the irregularity has a three-estimator partition, because the same pseudo-truth perturbation can be absorbed by ADML's Riesz report but not by DML's empirical AIPW report.

anchor_paper.author: Dukes, Vansteelandt, Whitney
anchor_paper.claim: Doubly robust DML inference can fail regularity when only one nuisance is consistently estimated.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Boundary/regime shift, not blocked: the new object is a three-estimator partition of irregularity rather than a CATE/ATT or iid-to-Markov extension.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from DukesVansteelandtWhitney2024JMLR because they already show irregularity under one-good-nuisance regimes.
seed_details.rebuttal: Their result is not a DML/LRO/ADML variance-report partition with exact ATE normalizations and pairwise spectral gaps.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work Dorn2025WeakOverlapDR studies thresholded weak-overlap doubly robust t-statistics. Our seed conjectures point-estimate equivalence can coexist with studentized-law separation for named variance rules, because thresholding changes the Riesz spectral scale but not the target ATE.

anchor_paper.author: Dorn
anchor_paper.claim: Weak overlap can destroy regular root-n estimation, and thresholded doubly robust t-statistics recover normal inference under stated rate costs.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Boundary/regime shift, not blocked: weak-overlap thresholding creates a named t-statistic report gap rather than merely weakening overlap.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from weak-overlap theory because thresholding already changes the variance rate.
seed_details.rebuttal: The seed compares three named variance rules after point-estimate equivalence, which weak-overlap thresholding papers do not jointly classify.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work AtheyImbensWager2018JRSSB emphasizes overlap-sensitive balancing weights. Our seed conjectures balancing-weight sensitivity is exactly the finite-support witness for DML/LRO/ADML report separation, because the residual-balance covariance kernel can have a growing smallest eigenvalue.

anchor_paper.author: Athey, Imbens, Wager
anchor_paper.venue: Journal of the Royal Statistical Society: Series B
anchor_paper.claim: Approximate residual balancing emphasizes that poor overlap makes ATE estimation sensitive to extrapolation and weighting choices.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Mechanism decomposition, not blocked: it turns overlap sensitivity into a finite-support spectral witness for named estimator reports.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from approximate residual balancing because balancing weights already encode overlap sensitivity.
seed_details.rebuttal: Approximate residual balancing is not a three-way DML/LRO/ADML studentization-report theorem under one binary-ATE normalization.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work ChernozhukovEscancianoIchimuraNeweyRobins2022LRO supplies misspecification-robust standard errors for locally robust GMM. Our seed conjectures misspecification-robust and automatic Riesz robust standard errors have a non-representability gap outside the Riesz projection class, because local robustness is not invariant to Jacobian normalization.

anchor_paper.author: Chernozhukov, Escanciano, Ichimura, Newey, Robins
anchor_paper.claim: LRO supplies locally robust semiparametric GMM moments and misspecification-robust standard errors.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Non-representability, not blocked: the target is a named LRO standard-error workflow and the obstruction is an open class outside the Riesz projection equivalence class.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from GMM sandwich theory because different Jacobians obviously give different variances.
seed_details.rebuttal: The seed requires an iff equivalence class tied to the ATE Riesz projection, not the generic fact that arbitrary GMM normalizations can differ.

proposed_from.literature_map:
Chernozhukov et al. (2018, The Econometrics Journal, ChernozhukovEtAl2018ECTJ) is the baseline DML source: cross-fitting plus Neyman-orthogonal scores for treatment and structural parameters under nuisance-rate conditions.
Chernozhukov, Escanciano, Ichimura, Newey, and Robins (2022, Econometrica, ChernozhukovEscancianoIchimuraNeweyRobins2022LRO) gives the broad locally robust semiparametric GMM construction.
Chernozhukov, Newey, and Singh (2022, Econometrica, ChernozhukovNeweySingh2022ADML) develops automatic debiasing for causal and structural regression effects and robust standard errors.
Chernozhukov, Newey, and Singh (2022, The Econometrics Journal, ChernozhukovNeweySingh2022RR) gives regularized Riesz representers, nonasymptotic uniform validity, and weak double-sparsity robustness.
Newey (1994, Econometrica, Newey1994Econometrica) and Robins-Rotnitzky-Zhao (1994, JASA, RobinsRotnitzkyZhao1994JASA) are the older semiparametric variance and augmented-IPW anchors.
Athey, Imbens, and Wager (2018, JRSSB, AtheyImbensWager2018JRSSB) supplies a balancing-weight comparison point and overlap-sensitivity motivation for ATE inference.
van der Laan, Luedtke, and Carone (2024/2025 arXiv, VanDerLaanLuedtkeCarone2024CDML) targets doubly robust asymptotic normality by calibrated DML when only one nuisance is good.
Dukes, Vansteelandt, and Whitney (2024, JMLR, DukesVansteelandtWhitney2024JMLR) shows doubly robust DML inference may fail regularity when only one nuisance is consistent.
Dorn (2025 working paper, Dorn2025WeakOverlapDR) studies how much weak overlap thresholded doubly robust t-statistics can handle.
Bruns-Smith, Dukes, Feller, and Ogburn (2024/2026, JRSSB/arXiv, BrunsSmithDukesFellerOgburn2024ABW) connects augmented balancing weights/AutoDML to linear regression equivalences.
Bruns-Smith (2026, arXiv, BrunsSmith2026RieszApproaches) compares direct Riesz estimation approaches and finds equivalence for ridge/RKHS settings but not necessarily for lasso or general ML classes.
The main tension is the absence of a sharp common-normalization theorem comparing equivalence, variance equivalence, and studentized-law equivalence for DML, LRO, and ADML under the same ATE law.
